hypothesis: Baseline trend-follow: long when the fast SMA is above the slow SMA.
rationale: Establish an honest, low-parameter trend benchmark — is there alpha beyond beta?
diagnosis: In-sample appraisal +0.21 collapses to OOS -0.25 (gap 0.45) with 2 free knob(s) over 4 combos on a 5yr IS window; 7/11 names go negative OOS; worst OOS fold 1967-01-02→1969-01-02 (appraisal -2.24, PG). Beta-adjusted alpha fit noise, not signal.
lesson: Ban sma_crossover: 2 knobs / 5yr IS curve-fit (gap 0.45). Cut to ≤2 knobs, lengthen IS to ≥6yr, and route to a different KIND of edge. Worst regime: 1967-01-02.
llm_lesson: Ban sma_crossover: 2 knobs / 5yr IS curve-fit (gap 0.45). Cut to ≤2 knobs, lengthen IS to ≥6yr, and route to a different KIND of edge. Worst regime: 1967-01-02.

hypothesis: Stack trend + RSI + momentum and tune four knobs hard on a 1-year window to maximize the in-sample number.
rationale: Chase the biggest backtest Sharpe — the classic curve-fitting trap.
diagnosis: In-sample appraisal +1.03 collapses to OOS -0.38 (gap 1.40) with 4 free knob(s) over 36 combos on a 1yr IS window; 9/11 names go negative OOS; worst OOS fold 1983-01-02→1984-01-02 (appraisal -2.66, XOM). Beta-adjusted alpha fit noise, not signal.
lesson: Ban multi_filter: 4 knobs / 1yr IS curve-fit (gap 1.40). Cut to ≤2 knobs, lengthen IS to ≥6yr, and route to a different KIND of edge. Worst regime: 1983-01-02.
llm_lesson: Ban multi_filter: 4 knobs / 1yr IS curve-fit (gap 1.40). Cut to ≤2 knobs, lengthen IS to ≥6yr, and route to a different KIND of edge. Worst regime: 1983-01-02.
carried_lesson_from_prev: Ban sma_crossover: 2 knobs / 5yr IS curve-fit (gap 0.45). Cut to ≤2 knobs, lengthen IS to ≥6yr, and route to a different KIND of edge. Worst regime: 1967-01-02.

hypothesis: Diversify the KIND of edge: buy oversold RSI, exit overbought (mean-reversion).
rationale: Trend overfit on this basket — try an uncorrelated mean-reversion edge.
diagnosis: OOS appraisal +0.15 (gap 0.56) over 12 combos / 4yr IS -- a genuine spark but thin or regime-dependent; 9/11 names hold OOS; worst OOS fold 2019-01-29→2021-01-29 (appraisal -1.49, SPY).
lesson: Tighten rsi_reversion: freeze knobs to sane defaults, lengthen IS to ≥6yr, demand positive OOS appraisal across more names before trusting it.
llm_lesson: Tighten rsi_reversion: freeze knobs to sane defaults, lengthen IS to ≥6yr, demand positive OOS appraisal across more names before trusting it.
carried_lesson_from_prev: Ban multi_filter: 4 knobs / 1yr IS curve-fit (gap 1.40). Cut to ≤2 knobs, lengthen IS to ≥6yr, and route to a different KIND of edge. Worst regime: 1983-01-02.

hypothesis: Apply the lesson: same mean-reversion edge, knobs frozen to sane defaults, long in-sample window, judged across the whole basket.
rationale: Trade in-sample dazzle for out-of-sample durability — discipline over knobs.
diagnosis: OOS appraisal +0.25 holds vs IS +0.33 (gap 0.09) across 191 folds; 8/11 names positive OOS -- beta-adjusted alpha survives walk-forward (Info-Ratio vs buy&hold is still negative: this is timing alpha, not a portfolio substitute).
lesson: Keep rsi_reversion as the trusted edge. Diversify to a different KIND of edge to find uncorrelated alpha; do not widen the grid.
llm_lesson: Keep rsi_reversion as the trusted edge. Diversify to a different KIND of edge to find uncorrelated alpha; do not widen the grid.
carried_lesson_from_prev: Tighten rsi_reversion: freeze knobs to sane defaults, lengthen IS to ≥6yr, demand positive OOS appraisal across more names before trusting it.
